current_entries.en_definition: A reduction in cost or expenditure.

examples.english: Some day you will come to realize the importance of saving.
examples.chinese: 你現在不好好儲蓄金錢的話，終有一天會後悔的。

current_entries.en_definition: (countable, usually, in the plural) Something (usually money) that is saved, particularly money that has been set aside for the future.
(informal) Shrewd, well-informed and perceptive.
(informal) To understand.
A tool with a toothed blade used for cutting hard substances, in particular wood or metal.

examples.english: I saw you with a tall boy.
examples.chinese: 我看見你和一個個子高的男孩在一起。

current_entries.en_definition: (informal, often, _, jocular, especially, _, used when referring to Japan) Goodbye, adieu.
A structure made of scaffolding for workers to stand on while working on a building.
Any of various marine bivalve molluscs of the superfamily Pectinoidea.
The part of the head where the hair grows from, or used to grow from.

examples.english: My scalp is very itchy.
examples.chinese: 我的頭皮非常癢。

current_entries.en_definition: A rascal, swindler, or rogue; a ne'er-do-well.
A mischievous person, especially a playful, impish youngster.
A permanent mark on the skin, sometimes caused by the healing of a wound.

examples.english: Will I have a scar?
examples.chinese: 我会留疤吗？

current_entries.en_definition: Scantily supplied (with); deficient (in); used with of.

examples.english: Telephone booths are as scarce as hen's teeth around here.
examples.chinese: 這裏附近的電話亭少得可憐。

current_entries.en_definition: A long, often knitted, garment worn around the neck.

examples.english: The girl had a long scarf around her neck.
examples.chinese: 女孩的脖子上圍著一條長長的圍巾。

current_entries.en_definition: (informal) To a scary extent; scarily.
(North America) An institution dedicated to teaching and learning; an educational institution.

examples.english: You did not come to school yesterday.
examples.chinese: 你昨天没来學校。

current_entries.en_definition: Of or pertaining to this genre.
(rare) One blade on a pair of scissors.
The act of scooping, or taking with a scoop or ladle; a motion with a scoop, as in dipping or shovelling.
The number of points accrued by each of the participants in a game, expressed as a ratio or a series of numbers.

examples.english: What was the score at halftime?
examples.chinese: 中场比分是多少？

current_entries.en_definition: The performance of an individual or group on an examination or test, expressed by a number, letter, or other symbol; a grade.
Twenty (20).
(music) The written form of a musical composition showing all instrumental and vocal parts.
A prostitute's client.
Contempt or disdain.
Of a thing or concept, of or pertaining to Scotland.

examples.english: I speak a little Scottish Gaelic.
examples.chinese: 我会说一点儿苏格兰盖尔语。

current_entries.en_definition: The Gaelic language of Scotland, spoken primarily in the northern and western parts of the country.
A member of any number of youth organizations belonging to the international scout movement, such as the Boy Scouts of America or Girl Scouts of the United States.
The wrinkling of the brows or face in frowning; the expression of displeasure, sullenness, or discontent in the countenance; an angry frown.
(slang) A fight, especially a fistfight without weapons.

examples.english: Please scrape the skin off the potatoes.
examples.chinese: 請把馬鈴薯的皮削掉。

current_entries.en_definition: Irregular, possibly illegible handwriting.
(computing) An image of computer or smartphone screen output at a given moment in time.
A layer of impurities that accumulates at the surface of a liquid (especially molten metal or water).
(derogatory, slang) A reprehensible person or persons.

(uncountable, disease) A disease caused by insufficient intake of vitamin C, leading to the formation of livid spots on the skin, spongy gums, loosening of the teeth, and bleeding into the skin and from almost all mucous membranes; (countable, obsolete) an occurrence of this disease.
A line or depression left by a cut or wound; a scar; a cicatrix.
(aircraft, nautical) Any aircraft capable of taking off from, and alighting on the surface of water.
(transitive) To char, scorch, or burn the surface of (something) with a hot instrument.

(North America, Australia, broadcasting) A group of episodes of a television or radio program broadcast in regular intervals with a long break between each group, usually with one year between the beginning of each.

examples.english: Winter is my favorite season.
examples.chinese: 冬天是我最喜欢的季节。

current_entries.en_definition: Of, related to, or reliant on a season or period of the year, especially with regard to weather characteristics.
(figuratively) A membership in an organization, particularly a representative body.

examples.english: Go back to your seat.
examples.chinese: 回到你的座位。

current_entries.en_definition: On the clock face of a clock or watch, the hand (pointer) that shows the number of seconds that have passed.
Next in order to the first or primary; of second place in origin, rank, etc.

examples.english: Must the woman always play the secondary role?
examples.chinese: 女性永遠只能扮演次要角色嗎？

current_entries.en_definition: Any small seed-like fruit.
(transitive) To try to reach or come to; to go to; to resort to.
A small spring, pool, or other spot where liquid from the ground (e.g. water, petroleum or tar) has oozed to the surface; a place of seeping.
A structure composed of a plank, balanced in the middle, used as a game in which one person goes up as the other goes down.
A length of some object.
An individual person as the object of the person's own reflective consciousness (plural selves).
not requiring external or additional support
(often, attributive) The practice of serving oneself, usually when purchasing items.

A visual system for transmitting information using the above equipment; especially, by means of two flags held one in each hand, using an alphabetic and numeric code based on the position of the signaller's arms; flag semaphore.

(programming) A bit, token, fragment of code, or some other mechanism which is used to restrict access to a shared function or device to a single process at a time, or to synchronize and coordinate events in different processes.
(chiefly, in the plural) A person from Senegal or of Senegalese descent.

(grammar) A grammatically complete series of words consisting of a subject and predicate, even if one or the other is implied. In modern writing, when using e.g. the Latin, Greek or Cyrillic alphabets, typically beginning with a capital letter and ending with a full stop or other punctuation. [from 15th c.]

examples.english: Please translate this sentence into Japanese.
examples.chinese: 請把這句話翻譯成日文。

current_entries.en_definition: The ninth month of the Gregorian calendar, following August and preceding October, containing the southward equinox.

examples.english: School starts in September in Europe.
examples.chinese: 歐洲的學校在九月開學。

current_entries.en_definition: Of or related to seventysomethings.
The South Slavic language of which Bosnian, Croatian, Montenegrin and Serbian are literary standards.
A love song that is sung directly to one's love interest, especially one performed below the window of a loved one in the evening.
time, place, circumstance
The ordinal form of the number seven.

examples.english: India is the seventh largest country in the world.
examples.chinese: 印度是世界第七大的國家。

current_entries.en_definition: The cardinal number immediately following seventy-four and preceding seventy-six.
Consisting of a number more than one (especially, more than two) but not very many. [from 17th c.]

examples.english: He came several times.
examples.chinese: 他来了很多次了。

current_entries.en_definition: The eighth month of the Islamic calendar.
(Islam) Iblis, Satan.
(informal) Instant, second. (Especially in two shakes.)

examples.english: I felt the house shake.
examples.chinese: 我感到房子在搖動。

current_entries.en_definition: (music) A rapid alternation of a principal tone with another represented on the next degree of the staff above or below it; a trill.
Trickery, hoaxing.

examples.english: With all its sham, drudgery and broken dreams; it is still a beautiful world.
examples.chinese: 虽然虚伪、苦痛还有破碎的梦伴随着我，但这仍然是一个美丽的世界。

current_entries.en_definition: To assume the manner and character of; to imitate; to ape; to feign.
An instance of washing the hair or other fibres with shampoo.
A major port city and direct-administered municipality of China, the largest urban area in China.

examples.english: The last time I went to China, I visited Shanghai.
examples.chinese: 我上次去过了中国，我访问了上海。

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive, copulative) To emit or reflect light so as to glow.

examples.english: Shine your shoes before going out.
examples.chinese: 出門前把你的鞋子擦亮。

current_entries.en_definition: Small, smooth pebbles, as found on a beach.
Having exceptional merit.

examples.english: Her eyes were shining with joy.
examples.chinese: 她的眼睛闪烁着喜悦的光芒。

current_entries.en_definition: (clothing) An article of clothing that is worn on the upper part of the body, and often has sleeves, either long or short, that cover the arms.

examples.english: Your shirt button is coming off.
examples.chinese: 你衬衣的纽扣快掉了。

current_entries.en_definition: (Hinduism) The god of destruction and transformation, and together with Brahma and Vishnu, one of the principal deities in Hinduism. Within Shaivism he is viewed as the Supreme deity, whereas in other branches of Hinduism such as the Smarta tradition he is worshipped as one of the six manifestations of the Divine.
The act of shivering.

examples.english: The mere thought of a snake makes me shiver.
examples.chinese: 一想到蛇就發顫。

current_entries.en_definition: A smooth tool that assists in putting the foot into a shoe, by sliding the heel in after the toe is in place.

examples.english: Do you have a shoehorn?
examples.chinese: 你有鞋拔子吗？

current_entries.en_definition: An establishment that sells goods or services to the public; originally only a physical location, but now a virtual establishment as well.

examples.english: There is a flower shop near by.
examples.chinese: 附近有一家花店。

current_entries.en_definition: A rough and rapid method of writing by substituting symbols for letters, words, etc.
(by extension) Any brief or shortened way of saying or doing something.

A hand tool with a handle, used for moving portions of material such as earth, snow, and grain from one place to another, with some forms also used for digging. In strict usage differentiated from a spade, which is designed solely for small-scale digging and incidental tasks such as chopping of small roots.
An instance of using of this device in order to bathe oneself.

examples.english: I usually take a shower in the evening.
examples.chinese: 我通常在晚上洗澡。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To cause to become smaller.
A shivering tremor, often from fear or horror.

examples.english: The terrible scene made him shudder.
examples.chinese: 恐怖的场面让了他颤抖。

current_entries.en_definition: (usually, in the plural) Protective panels, usually wooden, placed over windows to block out the light.
A lightweight object that is conical in shape with a cork or rubber-covered nose, used in badminton the way a ball is used in other racquet games. [from early 16th c.]
Thus; as written; used to indicate, for example, that text is being quoted as it is from the source.

examples.english: If you don't leave, I'll sic my dogs on you.
examples.chinese: 如果你不离开，我就放狗出来咬你。

current_entries.en_definition: (literally) Beside or alongside one another; close to one another and abreast.
A post bearing a sign that gives information on directions.

A cultural region of Central Europe, now chiefly in Poland, with small portions in the Czech Republic and Germany, from early modernity until 1945 predominantly German-speaking with Slavic-speaking minorities (and local majorities).
Mud or fine earth deposited from running or standing water.
Of or pertaining to apes and monkeys.
(heading, technical) Structurally uncomplicated.

examples.english: Math is like love: a simple idea, but it can get complicated.
examples.chinese: 数学就像爱情，一个简单的意思，但可以变得很复杂。

current_entries.en_definition: (algebraic, _, topology, geometry) A generalization of a triangle or tetrahedron to an arbitrary dimension, the generalization being the simplest possible convex polytope for a given dimension; more accurately, the convex hull of n+1 affinely independent points in n-dimensional space.
Of, pertaining to, or from Singapore, or its language or people.
The act of using the voice to produce musical sounds; vocalizing.

examples.english: He makes his living by singing.
examples.chinese: 他以唱歌為生。

current_entries.en_definition: A Sinhalese person: a member of the majority ethnic group of Sri Lanka.

current_entries.zh_definition: 僧伽羅語 僧伽罗语
current_entries.en_definition: The Indo-Aryan language of the Sinhalese people.
Of or pertaining to China or the Chinese peoples
(ergative) To descend or submerge (or to cause to do so) into a liquid or similar substance.
A bent pipe or tube with one end lower than the other, in which hydrostatic pressure exerted due to the force of gravity moves liquid from one reservoir to another.
A lord, master, or other person in authority, most commonly used vocatively: formerly in speaking to elders and superiors, later only when addressing a sovereign.
A male animal that has fathered a particular offspring (especially used of domestic animals and/or in biological research).
(sometimes, derogatory, colloquial) An effeminate boy or man.
(sometimes, derogatory, colloquial) A timid, unassertive or cowardly person.
The cardinal number immediately following sixty-seven and preceding sixty-nine.
(sexuality) A sex position where two people perform oral sex on each other simultaneously.
The cardinal number immediately following sixty-eight and preceding seventy.
The cardinal number immediately following sixty-five and preceding sixty-seven.
(pejorative, slang) A lewd and disreputable person, often female, especially an unattractive person with an air of tawdry promiscuity.
The act of roller skating or ice skating

examples.english: Can you skate?
examples.chinese: 你会溜冰吗？

current_entries.en_definition: (not comparable) Neither parallel nor perpendicular to a certain line; askew.

examples.english: His wig is skew.
examples.chinese: 他的假髮歪了。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To travel over (a slope, etc.) on skis; to travel on skis at (a place), (especially as a sport).
(nautical) The master of a ship.

examples.english: Let's call the dog Skipper.
examples.chinese: 讓我們叫這隻狗Skipper。

current_entries.en_definition: One of the wooden targets used in skittles.

Any of various small mammals, of most genera of the family Mephitidae, native to North and Central America, having a glossy black with a white coat and two musk glands at the base of the tail for emitting a noxious smell as a defensive measure.

examples.english: I almost stepped on a skunk last night.
examples.chinese: 我昨晚差点踩到一只臭鼬。

current_entries.en_definition: A small brown passerine bird, Alauda arvensis, that sings as it flies high into the air.
(intransitive) To rise or increase suddenly and extremely; to shoot up; to surge or spike.
(countable, sports) A course used for the sport of slalom.
Language outside of conventional usage and in the informal register.

examples.english: It's fun to learn slang words in foreign languages.
examples.chinese: 学习外语里的俚语很有趣。

current_entries.en_definition: A blow, especially one given with the open hand, or with something broad and flat.

examples.english: She gave him a slap in the face.
examples.chinese: 她打了他一巴掌。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive) To give a slap to.
A pair of sticks attached at one end and used to create a slapping sound effect, used especially in slapstick comedy; a type of clapper.
A member of any of the peoples of Europe who speak the Slavic languages.
A person who is held in servitude as the property of another person, and whose labor (and often also whose body and life) is subject to the owner's volition and control.

examples.english: I refuse to be treated like a slave by you.
examples.chinese: 我拒绝被你像奴隶一样对待。

current_entries.en_definition: A language spoken by the Slavey peoples.
Of the Slavs, their culture or the branch of the Indo-European languages associated with them.

examples.english: Slavic languages fascinate me.
examples.chinese: 斯拉夫语让我着迷。

current_entries.en_definition: (British) any type of sled or sleigh.

A hammer that consists of a large, heavy, broad and flat block of metal (the head) attached to a handle typically 0.5 meter to 1 meter long. The sledgehammer's design is meant to allow it to be swung powerfully, and to distribute force over a wide area upon impact.
A detective.
An item of play equipment that children can climb up and then slide down again.

Soft, moist earth or clay, having an adhesive quality; viscous mud; any substance of a dirty nature, that is moist, soft, and adhesive; bitumen; mud containing metallic ore, obtained in the preparatory dressing.
Any mucilaginous substance; or a mucus-like substance which exudes from the bodies of certain animals, such as snails or slugs.
A mistake or error.

examples.english: Tom talked too much and let the secret slip.
examples.chinese: 汤姆话太多，不小心把秘密泄露了出去。

current_entries.en_definition: (vulgar, _, slang) The vulva.

examples.english: Tom slit his own throat.
examples.chinese: 汤姆自刎了。

current_entries.en_definition: A narrow depression, perforation, or aperture; especially, one for the reception of a piece fitting or sliding into it.
Any of many gastropod mollusks, having no (or only a rudimentary) shell. [from early 18th c.]
An artificial passage for water, fitted with a valve or gate, for example in a canal lock or a mill stream, for stopping or regulating the flow.
(countable, pejorative) A dilapidated neighborhood where many people live in a state of poverty.

examples.english: Tom lives in a slum.
examples.chinese: 汤姆住在贫民窟里。

current_entries.en_definition: (intransitive) To be in a very light state of sleep, almost awake.
(originally, US, now, British, and, Irish) Wonderful, very good or impressive.

A facial expression comprised by flexing the muscles of both ends of one's mouth, often showing the front teeth, without vocalisation, and in humans is a common involuntary or voluntary expression of happiness, pleasure, amusement, goodwill, or anxiety.

examples.english: Your smile always makes me happy.
examples.chinese: 看見你笑，我就會高興起來。

current_entries.en_definition: That smiles or has a smile.

examples.english: This girl is always smiling, but she's not happy.
examples.chinese: 这个女孩总是微笑着，但她并不幸福。

current_entries.en_definition: (transitive, archaic) To dirty; to make dirty.
An uneven, often crooked smile that is insolent, self-satisfied, conceited or scornful.
(transitive, cooking) To cook in a close dish.
(figuratively) A problem or difficulty with something.
Any of very many animals (either hermaphroditic or nonhermaphroditic), of the class Gastropoda, having a coiled shell.

examples.english: The snail drew in its feelers.
examples.chinese: 蜗牛把触角缩了回去。

current_entries.en_definition: A mental or psychological trap.

examples.english: A fox is not caught twice in the same snare.
examples.chinese: 一只狐狸不会两次掉入同一个陷阱里。

current_entries.en_definition: (music) A set of stiff wires held under tension against the bottom head of a drum to create a rattling sound.
A knot or complication of hair, thread, or the like, difficult to disentangle.
(intransitive) To raise a corner of the upper lip slightly, especially in scorn.
An act of sneezing.

examples.english: I think I'm gonna sneeze. Give me a tissue.
examples.chinese: 我想我要打喷嚏了。。。给我张纸巾。

current_entries.en_definition: A cue sport, popular in the UK and other Commonwealth of Nations countries.
A brief period of sleep; a nap.
(ambitransitive) To breathe during sleep with harsh, snorting noises caused by vibration of the soft palate.

examples.english: I must admit that I snore.
examples.chinese: 我必須承認我打呼。

current_entries.en_definition: (underwater diving) A hollow tube, held in the mouth, or mounted on and opening into a diving mask, used by swimmers for breathing underwater.
Affected by snow blindness; temporarily blinded by light reflected off snow.

A ball of snow, usually one made in the hand and thrown for amusement in a snowball fight; also a larger ball of snow made by rolling a snowball around in snow that sticks to it and increases its diameter.

examples.english: We had a snowball fight.
examples.chinese: 我們打了一場雪球大戰。

current_entries.en_definition: A board, somewhat like a broad ski, or a very long skateboard with no wheels, used in the sport of snowboarding.
(vehicle) A vehicle with skis at the front and a caterpillar track at the rear, used for travelling over snow, sometimes as sport.
A flat item of footwear worn to facilitate walking in deep snow.
A deliberate affront or slight.
(transitive) To turn down insultingly; to dismiss.
Warm and comfortable; cosy.
(transitive, intransitive) To lie close to another person or thing, hugging or being cosy.
(informal) Goodbye; a valediction used when leaving or departing from a person or place.
(informal) Neither good nor bad; tolerable, passable, indifferent.
(intransitive) To be saturated with liquid by being immersed in it.
(countable, uncountable, informal, by extension, mineral) Some other substance, often a detergent or another surfactant, able to mix with both oil and water, used for cleaning.
(of a person) Tending to socialize or be social.

examples.english: I attended the party just to be sociable.
examples.chinese: 我去那個派對純粹是為了交際。

current_entries.en_definition: An order of Roman Catholic clergy, the Jesuits, having a tradition of education, theological scholarship, and missionary work; formed during the 16th century.
(clothing) A knitted or woven covering for the foot.

examples.english: There is a hole in your sock.
examples.chinese: 你的袜子上有个洞。

current_entries.en_definition: The stratum of the surface of the soil which is filled with the roots of grass, or any portion of that surface; turf; sward.

(biblical, Quranic) A city in the Middle East which, according to the Bible and Islamic tradition, but not specifically named in the Qur'an, was destroyed by God (along with Gomorrah) for the sins of its inhabitants.
A short stay somewhere.
to join items together, or to coat them with solder
(chiefly, countable, collective) An ethnic group native to the Somali Peninsula; a Somali, a group of Somalis, the Somalis.
One or some person of unspecified or indefinite identity.

examples.english: Hold on, someone is knocking at my door.
examples.chinese: 等等，有人在敲我的門。

current_entries.en_definition: Starting on one's feet, an instance of rotating one's body 360 degrees while airborne or on the ground, with one's feet passing over one's head.
At an indefinite but stated time in the past or future.
A fixed verse form of Italian origin consisting of fourteen lines that are typically five-foot iambics and rhyme according to one of a few prescribed schemes.
Fine black or dull brown particles of amorphous carbon and tar, produced by the incomplete combustion of coal, oil etc.
The musical part higher in pitch than alto, typically encompassing the range of the treble clef.
A member of a Slavic people living in Lusatia in eastern Germany.
The fruit of any of these trees, especially of the service tree.
A group of Slavic languages spoken by a minority in eastern Germany.
Any of various plants with acidic leaves of the genus Rumex, especially
A brown colour, with a tint of red.
unhappiness, woe

examples.english: No words can relieve her deep sorrow.
examples.chinese: 語言不能減輕她的悲痛。

current_entries.en_definition: (usually in plural) An instance or cause of unhappiness.
(intransitive, uncommon) To make a gloomy, sullen or miserable facial expression; to scowl.
A person from South Africa or of South African descent.
The continent forming the southern part of the Americas; east of the Pacific Ocean, west of the Atlantic Ocean, south of North America and north of Antarctica.
A citizen or resident of South Korea.

(historical) A workers' council, an institution first formed during the 1905 Russian Revolution and then instituted as the main form of communist government at all levels in the Soviet Union; by extension, a similar organization in early Chinese communism and elsewhere.

examples.english: Communism is the system practiced in the Soviet Union.
examples.chinese: 共產主義是在蘇聯被實行的制度。

current_entries.en_definition: (obsolete)

examples.english: Jane calls a spade a spade.
examples.chinese: 珍是一個有話直說的人。

current_entries.en_definition: (uncountable, rarely, countable, computing, Internet) Unsolicited bulk electronic messages.
Of or pertaining to the people or culture of Spain.

examples.english: I cannot speak English, much less Spanish.
examples.chinese: 我不會講英語，更不會講西班牙語。

current_entries.en_definition: A Romance language primarily spoken in Spain and in the Americas.
A thick pole or piece of wood.
A little spark; a scintillation.

examples.english: I count the sparkle of constellations to foretell the future of my love.
examples.chinese: 我數著星宿的星光來預測我愛情的未來。

current_entries.en_definition: A brief argument, falling out, quarrel.
(idiomatic, humorous) An expression sometimes used when a person mentioned in the current conversation happens to arrive on the scene.
(telephone) Indication that the person requested is the same as the one who is currently speaking.

examples.english: Whom are you speaking of?
examples.chinese: 你是指誰？

current_entries.en_definition: One's ability to communicate vocally in a given language.
A tiny spot or particle, especially of dirt.
The state of moving quickly or the capacity for rapid motion.

examples.english: The driver increased his speed.
examples.chinese: 司機加了速。

current_entries.en_definition: Aromatic or pungent plant matter (usually dried) used to season or flavor food.
(anatomy, zootomy) Of or relating to the spine.

A philosophical doctrine, established in France in the mid nineteenth century, postulating that humans are essentially immortal spirits that temporarily inhabit physical bodies, and may have influence on the physical world.
Of or pertaining to the spirit or the soul.

examples.english: Not material, but spiritual things determine our life.
examples.chinese: 我们的生活不是由物质，而是由精神来决定的。

current_entries.en_definition: Like a spoilsport.
An act of deception; a hoax; a joking prank. [from 1889]
A light parody. [from 1958]
Nonsense.
The act or result of spraining; lameness caused by spraining
(metalworking, countable) A side channel or branch of the runner of a flask, made to distribute the metal to all parts of the mold.

examples.english: Mary carries pepper spray in her purse.
examples.chinese: 瑪麗的包裡有辣椒噴霧。

current_entries.en_definition: A light rain shower.
A short race at top speed.
(informal) A potato. [from 1845]
(espionage) A person who secretly watches and examines the actions of other individuals or organizations and gathers information on them (usually to gain an advantage).

examples.english: It occurred to me that he might be an industrial spy.
examples.chinese: 我突然想到他可能是個商業間諜。

current_entries.en_definition: (often, nautical) A sudden storm, as found in a squall line.
(botany, countable) A plant and its fruit of any of a few species of the genus Cucurbita, or gourd kind.
(intransitive, slang) To give sensitive information about someone to a third party; to rat on someone.
(nautical) A long-handled tool used on ships for swabbing the decks and spreading protective coatings. [1844]
(architecture) A structure constructed between two adjacent walls to aid in the transition from a polygonal to a circular structure, as when a dome is constructed on top of a square room.
A title of dignity next in degree below knight, and above gentleman. See esquire.
A devoted attendant or follower of a lady; a beau.
A title of office and courtesy. See under esquire.
(UK, Irish, US, Australia, NZ, Philippines, slang) A small child.
(transitive, informal) To squeeze, compress, or crush (especially something moist).
A person from Sri Lanka or of Sri Lankan descent.
Relatively unchanging, steady, permanent; firmly fixed or established; consistent; not easily moved, altered, or destroyed.
A building, wing or dependency set apart and adapted for lodging and feeding (and training) ungulates, especially horses.
(plural staffs or staves) A long, straight, thick wooden rod or stick, especially one used to assist in walking.

examples.english: The fire broke out after the staff went home.
examples.chinese: 在全体职员回家后，火事爆发了。

current_entries.en_definition: (engineering) An arbor, as of a wheel or a pinion of a watch.
A discolored spot or area caused by spillage or other contact with certain fluids or substances.
A reagent or dye used to stain microscope specimens so as to make some structures visible.
A piece of wood or other material, usually long and slender, pointed at one end so as to be easily driven into the ground as a marker or a support or stay.

examples.english: I felt that my honor was at stake.
examples.chinese: 我覺得我的名譽受到了威脅。

current_entries.en_definition: (with definite article) The piece of timber to which a person condemned to death was affixed to be burned.
A share or interest in a business or a given situation.
Something used as a measure for comparative evaluations; a model.

examples.english: You have to learn standard English.
examples.chinese: 你必须得学标准英语。

current_entries.en_definition: The flag or ensign carried by a military unit.
(figurative) Position or reputation in society or a profession.
(Judaism) A symbol of Jewish identity and Judaism, having the shape of a hexagram, the compound of two equilateral triangles.
The national anthem of the United States of America.
(transitive) To excite by sudden alarm, surprise, or apprehension; to frighten suddenly and not seriously; to alarm; to surprise.
A collection, sometimes hidden; a reserve.
Dependable, loyal, reliable, trustworthy.
One of the bars or rounds of a rack, rungs of a ladder, etc; one of the cylindrical bars of a lantern wheel
(poetry) A metrical portion; a stanza; a staff.
(intransitive) To remain in a particular place, especially for a definite or short period of time; sojourn; abide.

examples.english: Why don't you stay a little while?
examples.chinese: 你為什麼不留下來一會兒？

current_entries.en_definition: A vessel in which articles are subjected to the action of steam, as in washing and in various processes of manufacture.
(transitive) To make a stereotype of someone or something, or characterize someone by a stereotype.

examples.english: It is a stereotype that police officers always eat doughnuts.
examples.chinese: 警察總吃甜甜圈是一種刻板印象。

current_entries.en_definition: (nautical) The rear part (after end) of a ship or other vessel.
(intransitive, figuratively) To suffer under uncomfortably hot conditions.

examples.english: The stew smells delicious.
examples.chinese: 這鍋墩湯聞起來真美味。

current_entries.en_definition: (religion, Christianity) bodily marks or sores, corresponding in location to the crucifixion wounds of Christ; supposed to occur during states of religious ecstasy or hysteria, and sometimes reported to bleed periodically
A period of time spent doing or being something; a spell.
A secret informant for police.

examples.english: He is only someone's stooge.
examples.chinese: 他无非是别人的跟屁虫。

current_entries.en_definition: A place where items may be accumulated or routinely kept.

examples.english: Please tell us where there is a grocery store.
examples.chinese: 请告诉我们哪儿有食品杂货店。

current_entries.en_definition: (obsolete) Proud; haughty.
Materially strong, enduring.
(geography) A narrow channel of water connecting two larger bodies of water.
A continuous series of like events.

(countable, physics) The internal distribution of force across a small boundary per unit area of that boundary (pressure) within a body. It causes strain or deformation and is typically symbolised by σ or τ.
Emotional pressure suffered by a human being or other animal.
A work stoppage (or otherwise concerted stoppage of an activity) as a form of protest.
A long region of a single colour in a repeating pattern of similar regions.
(in the plural) The badge worn by certain officers in the military or other forces.
A wandering on foot; an idle and leisurely walk; a ramble; a saunter.

examples.english: Spaniards love to stroll around in the evening cool.
examples.chinese: 西班牙人最愛在晚上外出散散步、乘乘涼。

current_entries.en_definition: A piece of certain paper items, designed to be torn off and kept for record or identification purposes.
(wiki) An article providing only minimal information and intended for later development.
(academic, transitive) To take a course or courses on a subject.

examples.english: I think it necessary for you to study harder.
examples.chinese: 我認為你需要更努力學習。

current_entries.en_definition: (informal) Miscellaneous items or objects; (with possessive) personal effects.
The tangible substance that goes into the makeup of a physical object.
(informal) Used as placeholder, usually for material of unknown type or name.
A fall, trip or substantial misstep.
The remains of something that has been cut off; especially the remains of a tree, the remains of a limb.
(drawing) An artists’ drawing tool made of rolled paper used to smudge or blend marks made with charcoal, Conté crayon, pencil or other drawing media.
A daring or dangerous feat, often involving the display of gymnastic skills.
To thwart or stump; to cause to fail or to leave hopelessly puzzled, confused, or stuck.
Abbreviation of submarine.
(chiefly, poetic, archaic) Of a person: dignified, majestic, noble.

examples.chinese: 他的武功已经达到了出神入化的境界。

Able to be submerged.

A succulent plant.
Lather; foam or froth formed by mixing soap and water.
The condition of someone who suffers; a state of pain or distress.

examples.chinese: 为什么生活充满着那么多痛苦?

current_entries.en_definition: (grammar, linguistic morphology) A morpheme added at the end of a word to modify the word's meaning.

examples.chinese: 下面是总统讲话的概要。
